FCFS.label: Classic FCFS
FCFS.description: Three processes arriving together — shows how arrival order determines scheduling.

SJF-NP.label: Simultaneous Arrival
SJF-NP.description: All processes arrive at time 0 — shortest burst time runs first.

SJF-NP.label: Varied Arrival Times
SJF-NP.description: Processes arrive at different times — scheduler picks the shortest available job.

SJF-P.label: Shortest Remaining Time First
SJF-P.description: Preemptive SJF — a new shorter process can interrupt the running one.

RR.label: Round Robin (Quantum = 20)
RR.description: Four processes sharing CPU time in fixed-size slices.

PS-P.label: Preemptive Priority
PS-P.description: Lower priority number = higher priority. A higher-priority arrival preempts the current process.

PS-NP.label: Non-Preemptive Priority
PS-NP.description: Lower priority number = higher priority. Once a process starts it runs to completion.
